free_text: Excitation of 0+,2+,0+,4+ analog states in the even
selenium isotopes with 19-25 MeV protons; coupled-
channel analysis
1H- ion beam.
Samples enriched to >97% in selenium isotopes
evaporated onto Mylar.
All data extracted using computer code which fitted
Gaussians to the observed peaks in the time spectra,
with the exception of the 4+ and 0+,2+ doublet in
80Se(p,n) and 82Se (p,n) at 19 MeV.  For these states,
the time spectra were converted to excitation energy
spectra and the areas of the 2-phonon peaks
integrated with a planimeter.
NE213 biased scintillators.
Data normalized to low beam current runs at 19, 22 and
25 MeV by comparing sum of counts in the ground state
analog neutron group at five forward angles.
Detector efficiency measured
using evaluated cross sections from Drosg.
Corrected for background.
Includes statistical counting uncertainties
and either fitting errors for computer fits or line
shape and background uncertainties for planimeter
integrations.
No reply from S.M. Grimes to author proof.
Data taken from Table I in reference.
